rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in component type scopes for assumptions and guarantees that reference visible ports and features.
Use implementation scopes for bindings and assignments that are valid only at the implementation level.
generic_rules: Ensure AGREE contracts are placed in valid AADL scopes based on whether they reference component types or implementations.
Only include assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they refer to visible ports and features.
Do not reference subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs.
Use visible identifiers from the AADL model to define AGREE expressions and constraints.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations; implementation annexes are for bindings and assignments.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type annexes unless the feature is explicitly represented in the type.
Do not reference subcomponent ports in component type contracts without proper variable representation.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
Model analyst extraction of AADL structure
